complexity.elements: Single element focus (one object, one character)
complexity.reasoning: None required; simple identification and labeling
complexity.context: Immediate physical environment; fully visual support

lexicalBoundary.complexity: Single words and formulaic chunks only.

sourceKtft.textDescription: Wordless story pictures; learner creates simple here-and-now descriptions using vocabulary targets as aids

pretask.name: Thematic Scene
pretask.description: View visual scene related to topic. Teacher points and names. Student identifies/labels 2-3 key elements.

inputtask.name: Listen-and-Do
inputtask.description: TPR commands or object selection. "Give me the cat." "Point to the red ball."

maintask.name: Listen-and-Select
maintask.description: Teacher describes; student selects from distractors. Simple information gap.

transfer.name: Show-and-Tell
transfer.description: Student shows their drawing/selection and names elements.

morphology: No verb inflection: jump, sit, run
vocabulary: Concrete nouns: cat, dog, ball, apple

level.title: White Dragon
level.description: Welcome to White Dragon — where every Kiddoland adventure begins! We'll learn our first English words through silly songs, picture stories, and games that make us laugh. By the time we're done, we'll be naming animals, telling tiny stories, and feeling proud of every word we say.

unit.title: Farm Animals
unit.bigQuestion: What animals live on the farm?
unit.description: In Farm Animals, we'll knock on the doors of Kiddoland's big red barn to meet all the friends who live there. We'll sing along with Barky the dog, learn a Dr-Seuss-style rhyme for every animal, and finish with a farm scavenger hunt that hides a secret message just for us!

protagonist.name: Barky
protagonist.description: A friendly black dog who lives in the big red barn.

unit.masterText: Barky lives on the farm. A cow says moo. A pig says oink. A sheep says baa. A horse says neigh. A duck says quack. A hen says cluck. A goat says maa. Barky barks. The farm is happy!

tinyTalk.target: L names 3 of 5 farm animals on Tiny Talk cards using single-word labels.

evaluationMonologue.target: L narrates the Farm Animals master text from picture cues, naming 8 of 10 animals and producing 5+ "It's a ___" frames.

sessions.title: Knock, Knock! Who's in the Barn?
sessions.littleQuestion: Who lives behind the barn doors?
sessions.description: Today we knock on the big red barn doors and meet Barky the farm dog! Behind every door is a new friend — a cow, a pig, a sheep — and we get to say their names out loud as each barn door swings open.

sessionAims.linguistic: Produce single-word labels for 5 farm animals (cow, pig, sheep, horse, duck).
sessionAims.cognitive: Match an auditory cue (T's word) to a visual referent (animal behind the barn door).

materials.name: Barn-Door Vocab Cards (cow, pig, sheep, horse, duck)
materials.purpose: Phase A vocabulary introduction; each card opens to reveal one animal

materials.name: Tiny Talk assessment card set
materials.purpose: End-of-session quick assessment; T points, L names

materials.name: What do you hear?
materials.purpose: Anchor song for the unit; opens every session

recasts.recast: T affirms then models: "Yes! It's a cow."
T models: "It IS a cow! It's a cow."
T exaggerates: "Sssshhheep," finger to lips, L echoes.

recasts.likelyError: L hesitates and points without naming.
recasts.recast: T offers binary choice: "Cow or pig?" L picks.

sessions.taskBrief: We open barn doors one at a time. I'll point at the door. You tell me what's inside. Then it's your turn to point — and I have to guess!
sessions.operationalMechanics: Five barn-door cards on the deck. T clicks one — door opens, animal revealed. T asks "What's this?" L produces "It's a ___." After 5 rounds T-leads, swap roles: L picks the door, T deliberately gets some wrong to provoke L correction. End with Tiny Talk assessment: 5 cards, L names 3+ to pass.

sessions.title: Barky's Big Story
sessions.littleQuestion: What story does Barky want to tell?
sessions.description: Barky has a story for us! Today we hear the Farm Animals tale for the very first time, with every animal making its own silly sound. We listen, we echo, and we follow Barky from door to door across Kiddoland's farm.

sessionAims.linguistic: Comprehend imperatives ("Give me the cow," "Point to the duck") and animal-sound pairings (cow-moo, pig-oink).
sessionAims.cognitive: Bind each animal to its signature sound; perform actions on auditory command.

materials.name: Barn-Door Vocab Cards (full set, 10 animals)
materials.purpose: Spread on screen for Listen-and-Do TPR commands

materials.name: Master Text Story Panels (10 illustrated panels)
materials.purpose: First reveal of Barky's story; T narrates, L echoes line by line

recasts.recast: T mimes confusion: "The cow? Or the horse?" L self-corrects.

recasts.likelyError: L names instead of acting on "Give me the cow."
recasts.recast: T models both: "You can SAY 'cow,' OR you can GIVE me the cow!"
T expands: "Give me the cow! Yes — give me the cow."

recasts.likelyError: L echoes story line incorrectly: "Cow moo."
recasts.recast: T models full frame: "A cow says moo." L echoes.

sessions.taskBrief: Barky has friends on the farm. I'll tell you what to do with them. Pick them up, point at them, hand them to me. Listen carefully — go!
sessions.operationalMechanics: 10 animal cards spread on the deck. T issues 12 TPR commands across 8 minutes ("Give me the duck," "Point to the goat," "Put the cow next to the pig"). L performs each. Track binary correct/incorrect on a visible scoreboard. After commands, T reveals master text via 10 illustrated story panels (cumulative — one panel per click), reading aloud, L echoes each line.

sessions.title: Tell It Back to Barky
sessions.littleQuestion: Can you tell Barky's story in your own words?
sessions.description: Now it's our turn! Barky listens while we tell the farm story ourselves, one animal at a time. The barn doors open one by one — and we name every friend before the next one shows up.

sessionAims.linguistic: Produce 2-3 word formulaic strings ("It's a cow," "Big pig," "A duck swims").
sessionAims.cognitive: Sequence pictures into a meaningful narrative; select correct referent under auditory distractor pressure.

materials.name: Cumulative Reveal Slides (animals accumulate one at a time)
materials.purpose: Phase B/C review mechanic; L names each new animal as it appears

recasts.recast: T raises hand: "Wait — listen ALL the clues!"
Accept at White Dragon level. No correction (per KTFT: no verb inflection).

recasts.likelyError: L can't sequence the cumulative reveal.
recasts.recast: T offers binary choice: "Cow first, or pig first?"
T models with emphasis: "It's a pig! It's. A. Pig."

sessions.taskBrief: I'll describe a farm friend. Don't shout the answer too fast — listen to ALL the clues, then pick the right one!
sessions.operationalMechanics: Cumulative reveal slides first: animals appear one at a time on a single scene; L names each new arrival, then re-names all visible animals. Then 6 rounds of Listen-and-Select. T reads description ("It is big. It is in the barn. It says moo.") — L scans visible animals, points at correct one. Information gap: T has knowledge L lacks. Difficulty escalates — later descriptions force L to wait for the third clue before deciding.

sessions.title: The Farm's Secret Message
sessions.littleQuestion: What is the farm trying to tell us?
sessions.description: It's the big day! Barky takes us on a farm scavenger hunt — every barn door, haystack, and pond hides an animal with a word. Find them all, put the words in the right order, and the farm's secret message appears!

sessionAims.linguistic: Produce full "It's a ___" frames under self-direction; read short single-word cues aloud.
sessionAims.cognitive: Decode a position-based message; reconstruct ordered output from numbered cues.

materials.name: Scavenger Map (8 numbered hotspots on a farm scene)
materials.purpose: Final task playing field; each hotspot hides an animal carrying a word

materials.name: Secret Message Word Strip (1=I, 2=LOVE, 3=THE, 4=FARM, 5=AND, 6=ALL, 7=ITS, 8=FRIENDS)
materials.purpose: Decoded payoff — L assembles the message from numbered cues

materials.name: Evaluation Monologue Picture Cues (master text panels, no captions)
materials.purpose: Final assessment; L narrates from images alone

recasts.likelyError: L skips ordering, just reads words at random.
recasts.recast: T points at numbers: "Wait — what number? Let's start at ONE."

recasts.likelyError: L reads message word-by-word with no flow.
recasts.recast: T models intonation; L echoes the whole sentence.
T amps up: "Two more! What's hiding here?!"

sessions.taskBrief: Every hotspot on the farm hides one of Barky's friends. They each have a word. Find them all, put the words in number order, and the farm's secret message will appear!
sessions.operationalMechanics: L clicks hotspots 1-8 on the farm scavenger map. Each click reveals an animal carrying a word. T tracks number→word on a sidebar. Once all 8 found, L assembles secret message in number order: "I LOVE THE FARM AND ALL ITS FRIENDS." L performs Show-and-Tell: holds up each animal in order, says "It's a [animal]" + the word it had. Final flourish: L reads the full secret message aloud. Then Evaluation Monologue — picture cues only, L narrates the story from memory.

Lyric column left, Barky-the-dog illustration right, music notes scattered, 'LET'S SING!' pink banner, click-me link.

slides.layout: 6 large primary-colored capital letters spaced across white space.
6 capital + lowercase pairs, primary colors, spaced evenly.
Same caps as slide 3, presented as recall.
Centered text on pastel background, Barky icon top-right.

content.secretMessage: I LOVE THE FARM AND ALL ITS FRIENDS.

slides.tPrompt: Now — put the words in order. What's the secret message?
slides.expectedLResponse: I love the farm and all its friends.

content.target: L narrates the full master text from images alone, 8/10 animals named, 5+ "It's a ___" frames produced.

slides.expectedLResponse: (L narrates master text from picture cues)

homework.title: Farm Animals
homework.readingText: Barky lives on the farm. A cow says moo. A pig says oink. A sheep says baa. A horse says neigh. A duck says quack. A hen says cluck. A goat says maa. Barky barks. The farm is happy!

listeningQuestions.audioText: I am big. I live in the barn. I say moo. What am I?
listeningQuestions.question: What is this?

listeningQuestions.audioText: I love the mud. I am pink. I say oink. What am I?
listeningQuestions.question: What is this?

listeningQuestions.audioText: I have feathers. I swim on the pond. I say quack. What am I?
listeningQuestions.question: What is this?

listeningQuestions.audioText: I jump high. I have horns. I say maa. What am I?
listeningQuestions.question: What is this?

homework.finalTask: Click each numbered hiding spot on Kiddoland's farm to find an animal carrying a word. Put all the words in number order to reveal the farm's secret message!
